title: Public Tech Media Lab Fellowship and Research Community at the University of Wisconsin–Madison: Join a Global Network Advancing Media, Technology, and Democracy
summary: The rapidly evolving relationship between technology, journalism, media, and democratic societies presents some of the most important challenges and opportunities of our time. From artificial intelligence and digital platforms to data governance, misinformation, civic engagement, and public interest technology, addressing these issues requires collaboration across disciplines, professions, and communities. The Public Tech Media Lab (PTML) at the University of Wisconsin–Madison is inviting applications from scholars, researchers, journalists, technologists, academic staff, and community leaders who are passionate about exploring and shaping the future of media, technology, and democracy. PTML offers a unique interdisciplinary environment where members can collaborate on innovative research, participate in thought-provoking discussions, access funding opportunities, and contribute to projects that advance the public good. For individuals seeking a vibrant intellectual community focused on public interest technologies and their impact on journalism and democratic life, joining the Public Tech Media Lab represents an exciting opportunity to connect with experts across multiple fields while contributing to meaningful research and practice. About the Public Tech Media Lab The Public Tech Media Lab is housed at the University of Wisconsin–Madison and serves as a collaborative hub dedicated to understanding, designing, and implementing technologies that support journalism, democratic participation, and the public interest. The Lab is built on a core belief: No single discipline can adequately address the increasingly complex challenges posed by emerging technologies and their influence on media ecosystems and democratic institutions. As a result, PTML brings together experts from a wide variety of academic and professional backgrounds to develop interdisciplinary solutions and generate new knowledge at the intersection of: Technology Journalism Media Democracy Public Policy Civic Engagement Public Interest Innovation The Lab creates opportunities for collaboration, experimentation, research, and practical engagement among individuals who share a commitment to strengthening democratic societies through responsible technological development. Why the Public Tech Media Lab Matters Technology increasingly shapes how people access information, engage with public affairs, and participate in democratic processes. Key issues facing societies today include: Artificial intelligence in journalism Information integrity Misinformation and disinformation Digital platform accountability Media trust and credibility Data privacy and governance Public participation in technology design Ethical technology development Civic technology innovation Addressing these challenges requires expertise from many different sectors and disciplines. The Public Tech Media Lab serves as a platform where these diverse perspectives can come together to produce impactful research and practical solutions. Who Can Join the Public Tech Media Lab? PTML welcomes individuals from a broad range of backgrounds. Potential members include: Graduate Students Graduate students interested in exploring the intersection of technology, media, and democracy can participate in collaborative research initiatives and connect with leading scholars and practitioners. Faculty Members Faculty members whose teaching and research align with PTML s mission are encouraged to apply and become part of a growing interdisciplinary network. Academic Staff Academic professionals engaged in teaching, research, outreach, and public engagement activities related to media and technology can contribute valuable expertise to the Lab community. Journalists Journalists seeking to better understand technological change and its effects on news production, distribution, and public trust can engage with researchers and technologists through PTML initiatives. Technologists Technology professionals interested in public interest technology, ethical design, and democratic innovation can collaborate with scholars and media experts on real-world challenges. Community Partners Organizations and individuals working within communities can help ensure that research and technological development remain responsive to public needs and societal concerns. Areas of Expertise and Disciplines Represented The Lab actively encourages applications from individuals with backgrounds in: Media and Communication Fields Journalism Media Studies Communication Digital Media Social Sciences Political Science Sociology Public Policy International Affairs Technology and Innovation Computer Science Artificial Intelligence Data Science Human-Computer Interaction Legal and Governance Fields Law Digital Rights Technology Policy Information Governance Design and User Experience Design Research User-Centered Design Civic Design Digital Innovation The interdisciplinary nature of PTML is one of its greatest strengths, enabling members to learn from perspectives outside their primary area of expertise. Faculty and Academic Staff Associate Opportunities The Public Tech Media Lab specifically welcomes applications from faculty members and academic staff who demonstrate a strong commitment to the Lab s mission. Applicants may qualify if they: 1. Teach Relevant Courses Faculty and staff who teach courses addressing themes related to: Journalism Media Technology Democracy Public Interest Innovation are encouraged to apply. 2. Conduct Relevant Research Researchers whose work explores the relationship between media, technology, public life, democratic participation, or civic engagement may be strong candidates for membership. 3. Demonstrate Commitment to PTML s Mission Applicants who show an intellectual, pedagogical, or practical commitment to advancing interdisciplinary understanding of media and technology are welcomed into the community. Benefits of Becoming a PTML Member Membership in the Public Tech Media Lab offers numerous professional and academic advantages. 1. Interdisciplinary Networking Opportunities Members gain access to a diverse community of scholars, practitioners, journalists, policymakers, and technologists. This environment promotes: Knowledge sharing Collaborative problem-solving Interdisciplinary learning Cross-sector partnerships 2. Access to Funding Opportunities PTML members may become eligible to apply for Lab-supported funding opportunities that can help advance research projects, collaborative initiatives, and innovative experiments. 3. Research Collaboration Members can participate in collaborative research initiatives that address pressing issues at the intersection of technology, media, and democracy. These collaborations often involve: Academic researchers Industry professionals Journalists Public institutions Community organizations 4. Opportunities to Present Research PTML programming provides opportunities for members to: Present ongoing research Share project findings Receive feedback from peers Engage in scholarly discussions 5. Engagement with Journalists and Policymakers Members benefit from direct interaction with: Journalists Technology experts Government officials Policy leaders Civic innovators These connections can expand the reach and impact of research and public engagement efforts. 6. Invitations to Workshops and Events PTML hosts a variety of events designed to stimulate interdisciplinary dialogue and collaboration. These may include: Research seminars Workshops Guest lectures Networking events Public discussions Collaborative working groups 7. Supportive Intellectual Community Perhaps the greatest benefit of membership is becoming part of a supportive community of like-minded individuals who share a commitment to advancing knowledge and public interest outcomes through technology and media innovation. Building the Future of Public Interest Technology The Public Tech Media Lab seeks to foster a future where technological development serves democratic values and public needs. By bringing together experts from different disciplines and sectors, the Lab creates opportunities to: Develop innovative research agendas Explore emerging technologies responsibly Strengthen journalism ecosystems Improve democratic participation Advance public interest technology Create meaningful societal impact For individuals passionate about shaping the future of media, technology, and democracy, PTML offers a dynamic environment for collaboration, learning, and leadership. Deadline: Apply by 1 August 2026 How to Apply Interested individuals can submit an application through the Public Tech Media Lab s membership application process. Prospective members are encouraged to review the Lab s mission, research priorities, and membership expectations before applying. Faculty, academic staff, graduate students, journalists, technologists, and community partners who share PTML s vision of advancing media, technology, and democracy through interdisciplinary collaboration are strongly encouraged to apply. Apply here For more information, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Public Tech Media Lab Fellowship and Research Community at the University of Wisconsin–Madison: Join a Global Network Advancing Media, Technology, and Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Future Generation Art Prize 2027: Win Up to US$100,000 and Gain Global Recognition as a Young Contemporary Artist
summary: The Future Generation Art Prize 2027 is now officially accepting applications from talented young artists around the world. Recognized as one of the most prestigious international art awards for emerging artists, this global prize offers an exceptional opportunity for creatives aged 35 and under to gain international exposure, receive substantial financial support, and showcase their work on one of the world s most respected contemporary art platforms. Established by the Victor Pinchuk Foundation in 2009 and organized through the PinchukArtCentre in Kyiv, Ukraine, the Future Generation Art Prize was created to discover, recognize, and support the next generation of influential contemporary artists. Over the years, the prize has helped launch and strengthen the careers of more than 100 artists through exhibitions, mentorship opportunities, global exposure, and financial support. What is the Future Generation Art Prize? The Future Generation Art Prize is a biannual international contemporary art prize that identifies exceptional artistic talent from every corner of the globe. Unlike many art competitions that focus on specific regions or artistic disciplines, this prize is completely open and accessible to artists worldwide, regardless of their nationality, race, gender, or artistic medium. The initiative operates through a transparent online application process, making it one of the most democratic and globally accessible art prizes available today. Through its extensive network of over 60 partner platforms and hundreds of international art correspondents, the prize reaches artists from diverse cultural and artistic backgrounds. Prize Benefits and Awards One of the most attractive aspects of the Future Generation Art Prize is its substantial financial support package. Main Prize The overall winner will receive a total award worth US$100,000 , divided into: US$60,000 cash prize US$40,000 investment toward the artist’s practice and future artistic production This unique structure ensures that artists not only receive immediate financial recognition but also obtain resources to continue developing ambitious artistic projects. Special Prizes In addition to the main award: Up to five Special Prizes may be awarded by the international jury. A total of US$20,000 is available for special prize recipients. These awards are intended to support projects that contribute to the long-term development of the artists’ practices. Why Artists Should Apply The Future Generation Art Prize offers much more than financial rewards. Successful applicants gain access to: 1. Global Visibility Shortlisted artists receive international exposure through exhibitions and media coverage, helping them establish a global artistic presence. 2. Exhibition Opportunities Selected artists will be commissioned to create new works that will be exhibited at the PinchukArtCentre in Kyiv, providing a significant platform for showcasing their talent. 3. Career Development The prize has a long history of supporting artists beyond the award itself, helping them secure future opportunities, collaborations, and recognition within the international art community. 4. International Networking Artists become part of a prestigious global network of curators, critics, collectors, educators, and fellow artists. Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet the following criteria: Be 35 years old or younger Work in any artistic medium Submit an online application Comply with all application requirements Former prize winners are not eligible, but previous applicants may reapply if they continue to meet the eligibility criteria. There are no restrictions based on: Nationality Race Gender Geographic location Artistic discipline or medium Selection Process The competition follows a rigorous international selection process. Stage 1: Application Review A distinguished selection committee reviews all submitted applications and evaluates artistic quality, originality, and potential. Stage 2: Shortlisting Up to 20 artists are selected for the official shortlist, while the winner of the PinchukArtCentre Prize automatically becomes the 21st nominee. Stage 3: Exhibition Shortlisted artists are invited to present newly commissioned works during an exhibition at the PinchukArtCentre. Stage 4: Award Ceremony An international jury selects the final winner and special prize recipients during the Future Generation Art Prize 2027 award ceremony. Application Requirements To complete an application, artists should prepare: Portrait photograph Curriculum Vitae (CV) Biography Exhibition history Descriptions of artistic works Photos and/or videos of artworks A minimum of three artistic works for submission Important Dates Event Date Applications Open 11 May 2026 Application Deadline 28 June 2026 Shortlist Announcement 14 September 2026 Exhibition of Shortlisted Artists Spring 2027 Award Ceremony Spring 2027 How to Apply Interested artists can submit their applications online through the official Future Generation Art Prize application portal . Interested applicants can learn more about this opportunity on Official Website With a life-changing US$100,000 award, international recognition, prestigious exhibition opportunities, and a commitment to supporting artistic growth, the Future Generation Art Prize 2027 represents one of the most significant opportunities available to young contemporary artists worldwide. Artists with innovative ideas, compelling artistic visions, and the ambition to showcase their work on a global stage are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline of 28 June 2026 Discover more global award opportunities on OFY here The post Future Generation Art Prize 2027: Win Up to US$100,000 and Gain Global Recognition as a Young Contemporary Artist appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HCSS General Internship 2026 in the Netherlands: Paid Opportunity for Students Interested in Security, Defence, and Geopolitics
summary: The Hague Centre for Strategic Studies (HCSS) has officially opened applications for its highly sought-after General Internship Programme, with successful candidates expected to begin their internships between the end of July and mid-August 2026. This prestigious internship offers students a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in one of Europe s leading think tanks specializing in security, defence, geopolitics, international affairs, and strategic policy research. For students looking to launch careers in research, policy analysis, international relations, defence studies, diplomacy, or public affairs, the HCSS General Internship represents an exceptional opportunity to work alongside experienced researchers and policy experts while contributing directly to projects addressing some of the world s most pressing security and geopolitical challenges. About The Hague Centre for Strategic Studies (HCSS) The Hague Centre for Strategic Studies is a renowned independent think tank based in The Hague, Netherlands. HCSS conducts research and analysis on critical issues affecting global security, international relations, defence policy, technology, energy security, and geopolitical developments. The organization works closely with governments, international organizations, policymakers, businesses, and academic institutions to provide evidence-based research and strategic insights. Through its internship programme, HCSS enables talented students to gain direct exposure to policy-oriented research and strategic analysis in a professional environment. Why Apply for the HCSS General Internship? The internship is designed to provide students with practical experience that goes beyond traditional academic learning. Interns become integrated members of research teams and contribute to ongoing projects while developing valuable professional skills. Benefits of the internship include: 1. Paid Internship Opportunity Selected interns receive a monthly stipend of €675 , making this a paid internship opportunity that allows students to gain professional experience while receiving financial support. 2. Hands-On Research Experience Interns actively participate in research activities, including: Conducting background research Collecting and analyzing data Supporting policy analysis Preparing research materials Assisting with publications and reports Contributing to strategic studies projects 3. Professional Development Participants develop highly sought-after skills such as: Policy analysis Critical thinking Strategic research Academic writing Data analysis Communication skills Project management Professional networking 4. Networking Opportunities Interns work closely with leading experts, researchers, policymakers, and professionals from various sectors, helping them build valuable professional networks that can support future career growth. 5. Exposure to International Affairs The programme offers direct exposure to contemporary issues involving: International security Defence policy Geopolitics Foreign policy Strategic affairs Emerging global challenges Internship Responsibilities As a General Intern at HCSS, participants may be involved in a wide range of activities depending on project needs and team assignments. Typical responsibilities include: Supporting ongoing research projects Conducting literature reviews Gathering and analyzing data Assisting with report writing Contributing to academic papers Organizing workshops and conferences Providing administrative and project support Supporting event management activities Participating in policy-focused discussions and research initiatives Eligibility Requirements To be eligible for the HCSS General Internship Programme, applicants must meet several requirements. Academic Requirements Candidates should possess: An excellent academic background Strong analytical abilities Excellent writing skills Strong synthesis and research capabilities Interest in HCSS research themes and topics Professional Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Strong communication skills in English Ability to think creatively and critically Knowledge of advanced internet research methods Ability to work independently Flexibility and adaptability Positive professional attitude Strong work ethic Willingness to learn and contribute actively Additional Advantages The following qualifications are considered beneficial: Previous internship or work experience Knowledge of Dutch or additional languages Native-level English proficiency Quantitative research skills Experience with Python or R programming Data science experience Important Eligibility Conditions Applicants must note several critical requirements: University Enrollment Candidates must remain officially enrolled at a university throughout the entire internship period. Recent graduates who are no longer students are not eligible. Internship Duration HCSS strongly prefers interns who can commit to a six-month internship period. However, shorter durations may be discussed under specific circumstances. Full-Time Commitment Interns are expected to work: 40 hours per week In person at the HCSS office in The Hague Throughout the agreed internship period Work Authorization Requirement Applicants must already possess all necessary documentation required to study or work in the Netherlands before applying. HCSS does not provide: Visa sponsorship Work permits Immigration support documents Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents in one PDF file: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover Letter Proof of University Enrollment Academic Transcripts One-page Writing Sample responding to one of the questions provided by HCSS References with contact information (minimum two references) Incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Dates Activity Date Internship Start Period End of July – Mid-August 2026 Application Deadline 5 July 2026 (12:00 PM) Interviews Approximately 2–3 weeks after deadline Notification of Results Around one month after closing date How to Apply Interested students should prepare all required documents and submit their application as a single PDF file to: Email: internship@hcss.nl Subject Line: General Internship /2026 Applicants should ensure that all documents are written in English and submitted according to the application instructions provided by HCSS . Only complete applications meeting all eligibility requirements will be reviewed. Students passionate about international security, strategic studies, defence policy, and geopolitics are strongly encouraged to apply. The HCSS General Internship Programme offers an outstanding opportunity to gain practical experience, expand professional networks, and build a strong foundation for future careers in research, policy, government, international organizations, and the private sector. With a paid monthly stipend, direct involvement in impactful projects, and access to a leading European think tank, this internship can serve as a significant stepping stone toward a successful international career. Discover more global internships opportunities on OFY here The post HCSS General Internship 2026 in the Netherlands: Paid Opportunity for Students Interested in Security, Defence, and Geopolitics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women – UN Women JPO Opportunity in Dakar, Senegal (2026 Intake)
summary: The Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women (EVAW) position under the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) programme with UN Women represents one of the most impactful early-career international development opportunities available in 2026. Based in Dakar, Senegal , this role is designed for young professionals who are committed to advancing gender equality, eliminating violence against women and girls, and contributing to global development efforts through the United Nations system. This opportunity is managed through the Nedworc Foundation , which coordinates Junior Professional Officer placements across various UN agencies. Applications for this prestigious role close on 28 June 2026 . Overview of the Opportunity The Programme Analyst position sits within UN Women’s Ending Violence Against Women (EVAW) Section , which focuses on designing and implementing programmes that address violence against women and girls across Africa. Violence against women and girls remains one of the most widespread human rights challenges globally, and this role contributes directly to regional and continental solutions. The selected candidate will work on: Programme planning and implementation Policy development and advocacy Multi-country and regional coordination Partnerships with governments, UN agencies, and civil society Monitoring and evaluation of gender-based violence programmes Support for large-scale global initiatives such as the Spotlight Initiative A major focus of the role is contributing to the Spotlight Initiative , a landmark partnership between the United Nations and the European Union aimed at eliminating all forms of violence against women and girls. Key Responsibilities The Programme Analyst will be expected to contribute to multiple areas of work within the EVAW Section: 1. Programme Development and Implementation Supporting the design of EVAW programmes across Africa Assisting in the implementation of regional and country-level initiatives Ensuring alignment with UN Women’s strategic objectives 2. Policy and Advocacy Work Supporting policy reforms related to gender equality Assisting governments in strengthening legal frameworks on gender-based violence Contributing to advocacy campaigns and awareness initiatives 3. Partnership Coordination Collaborating with UN agencies and development partners Engaging with civil society organizations and women’s rights groups Supporting multi-sectoral coordination efforts 4. Monitoring and Evaluation Collecting and analyzing programme data Assessing programme impact and effectiveness Contributing to reporting and documentation processes 5. Support to Global Initiatives Assisting in implementation of the Spotlight Initiative Supporting EVAW flagship programme development Contributing to multi-country programming strategies Development Focus Areas This role contributes to several interconnected development priorities across Africa: Ending Violence Against Women and Girls The primary focus is to prevent and respond to all forms of violence affecting women and girls, including: Domestic violence Sexual violence Harmful traditional practices Institutional and systemic gender inequality Reproductive and Maternal Health Collaboration The EVAW Section works with UN partners on broader health-related programming, including: Reproductive health access Maternal and neonatal health Child and adolescent health services Multi-Agency UN Collaboration The role also contributes to initiatives such as: Muskoka Initiative H6 partnership framework Cross-agency gender equality programming Why This Opportunity Matters The Junior Professional Officer programme is one of the most respected entry routes into the United Nations system. This role offers: Direct exposure to international development programming Hands-on experience with UN policy and field operations Opportunities to work with global experts and policymakers Experience in high-impact gender equality programming Strong career progression pathways in international organizations Application Deadline The closing date for applications is: 28 June 2026 Early application is strongly recommended due to high competition. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet strict eligibility criteria: Age Requirement Must be born after 1 January 1994 Must be 32 years or younger by 31 December 2026 Educational Requirements Applicants must hold either: Master’s Degree Gender Studies, Development Studies, Economics, Sociology, International Relations, or related fields Minimum 2 years relevant experience OR Bachelor’s Degree Minimum 4 years relevant professional experience Academic Background Requirement A significant portion of academic studies must have been completed in an eligible country listed by the programme Nationality Requirement Applicants must be nationals of eligible countries only Nationals of Senegal are not eligible due to the duty station being in Dakar Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for individuals who: Are passionate about gender equality and women’s rights Have experience in development, social policy, or human rights work Are interested in UN systems and international cooperation Have strong analytical and communication skills Are committed to addressing gender-based violence globally Eligible Applicants: Region and Background Geographic Eligibility Applications are open to candidates from: Eligible countries listed under the Nedworc Foundation JPO scheme Countries across Africa, Asia, Latin America, Eastern Europe, and other developing regions (excluding Senegal for this post) Professional Background Strong applicants typically come from: Gender Studies International Development Sociology Political Science Law or Human Rights Economics or Social Policy Application Learn more and apply here Category of Opportunity Job (Junior Professional Officer – UN Women Programme Analyst Position) Category of Qualified Candidates Early-career professionals (typically under 32 years old) Holders of Bachelor’s or Master’s degrees in social sciences or development-related fields Candidates from eligible countries listed under the UN JPO programme (excluding Senegal for this posting) Individuals with 2–4+ years of relevant professional experience depending on qualification level Professionals interested in gender equality, human rights, and international development careers For more information, visit the official website Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women – UN Women JPO Opportunity in Dakar, Senegal (2026 Intake) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WUSC DREEM Deputy Project Director Job 2026: Lead a Pan-African Initiative Empowering One Million Refugee and Displaced Youth Across Africa
summary: The World University Service of Canada (WUSC) is seeking an exceptional bilingual development professional to join its flagship DREEM initiative as Deputy Project Director (DPD). This senior leadership opportunity offers experienced professionals the chance to contribute to one of Africa’s most ambitious refugee and displaced youth empowerment programs. The position supports the implementation of the second phase of the DREEM initiative (2026–2031), a transformative Pan-African programme that aims to significantly improve education and employment opportunities for refugee, displaced, and host community youth, particularly young women. The successful candidate will play a central role in shaping strategy, managing technical teams, coordinating partnerships, and driving implementation across multiple African countries. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply early. About WUSC World University Service of Canada (WUSC) is a leading Canadian international development organization dedicated to creating positive educational and economic opportunities for young people around the world. WUSC’s vision is a world where every young person thrives and feels a sense of belonging. Through innovative partnerships and locally driven solutions, the organization works to ensure that young people can realize their aspirations and build meaningful futures. Headquartered in Ottawa, Canada, WUSC operates across Africa, Asia, the Caribbean, Latin America, and the Middle East. The organization is recognized globally for delivering sustainable development programs that create measurable and lasting impact. WUSC offers a dynamic multicultural working environment that brings together professionals from diverse backgrounds who share a commitment to social justice, inclusion, and youth empowerment. About the DREEM Initiative In January 2026, WUSC launched Phase II of the DREEM programme, which will run until 2031. The initiative focuses on supporting refugee, displaced, and host community youth (RDP youth) across Africa by helping them access dignified employment opportunities and quality education. Building on the achievements of its first phase, DREEM Phase II seeks to expand its reach into new geographic areas while strengthening local systems that support refugee and displaced populations. Vision and Goals of DREEM Phase II The programme has set highly ambitious targets for the next five years, including: Indirectly enabling one million refugee, displaced, and host community youth to secure dignified employment opportunities. Supporting 35,000 young people to access education opportunities. Strengthening the inclusion of refugee and displaced youth in economic systems. Enhancing access to higher education and technical education pathways. Building sustainable local capacity through Refugee-Led Organizations (RLOs). The programme places a strong emphasis on supporting young women and promoting gender-responsive development approaches. The Strategic Role of Refugee-Led Organizations (RLOs) A defining feature of DREEM Phase II is its commitment to localization and refugee leadership. The initiative recognizes Refugee-Led Organizations as critical actors in creating sustainable and community-driven solutions. The programme will support RLOs through three key mechanisms: 1. RLO Opportunity Fund Providing direct financial access and support to Refugee-Led Organizations. 2. Capacity Strengthening Equipping RLOs with technical expertise and organizational development support. 3. Integrated Participation Embedding RLOs throughout programme implementation and decision-making processes. This strategy aims to create sustainable local ownership and long-term impact. Position Overview: Deputy Project Director The Deputy Project Director serves as the second-in-command of the DREEM programme and works closely with the Project Director to ensure successful programme implementation and achievement of results. The DPD will support strategic planning, operational oversight, staff management, partnership coordination, and technical leadership at the Pan-African level. The role is ideal for a visionary leader who combines technical expertise with strong people management and partnership-building skills. Preferred Duty Stations WUSC offers flexibility regarding the candidate s duty station. Preference will be given to candidates based in: Nairobi, Kenya Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire Accra, Ghana Kigali, Rwanda Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Lagos, Nigeria The successful candidate must already possess legal work authorization in the country where they intend to be based. No relocation allowance will be provided. Key Technical Areas of Responsibility The Deputy Project Director will oversee three major technical pillars of the programme. 1. Refugee-Led Organization Engagement Supporting localization efforts and strengthening refugee-led institutions. 2. Technical Assistance Mechanism Providing technical support to partners across the DREEM ecosystem. 3. Higher Education Inclusion Expanding access to higher education opportunities for refugee and displaced youth. The DPD will directly supervise Senior Technical Managers responsible for these pillars as well as the Training and Curriculum Development Specialist. Core Responsibilities The successful candidate will be responsible for a wide range of leadership, technical, and operational functions. Strategic Programme Leadership Responsibilities include: Ensuring project activities achieve annual workplan commitments. Supporting adaptive management and programme quality assurance. Driving implementation of programme objectives across Africa. Staff Leadership and Capacity Building The Deputy Project Director will: Mentor and coach programme staff. Strengthen competencies in project management and partnership development. Build staff capacity around refugee inclusion approaches. Training and Technical Support Responsibilities include: Developing training materials and capacity-building tools. Designing coaching and mentoring interventions. Supporting organizational learning initiatives. Partnership Development and Stakeholder Engagement The role involves: Building relationships with implementing partners. Coordinating with consultants and subcontractors. Managing strategic donor relationships. Collaborating with government stakeholders and institutions. Donor Coordination The Deputy Project Director will work closely with: Mastercard Foundation WUSC headquarters in Canada African country teams Youth Advisory Board Youth Technical Committee The DPD may also represent the programme when required. Programme Planning and Management Additional responsibilities include: Budget development Annual work planning Project reporting Monitoring implementation performance Ensuring compliance with donor requirements Qualifications and Experience Required WUSC is seeking a highly qualified professional with extensive experience in international development and refugee inclusion. Education Candidates must possess: A Master s Degree in: International Development Education Public Policy Social Sciences Or a related discipline Additional project management certifications such as PMP or PRINCE2 are considered strong assets. Language Requirements Fluency in both English and French is mandatory. This bilingual requirement reflects the programme s Pan-African scope and the need to work effectively across Anglophone and Francophone countries. Sector Expertise Candidates should demonstrate substantial experience in one or more of the following sectors: Forced displacement Refugee inclusion Inclusive market systems Higher education International development Specialized Technical Expertise Applicants should possess expertise in at least one of the following areas: Localization and organizational strengthening Refugee-Led Organization development Refugee inclusion in higher education Technical assistance design and delivery Leadership and Project Management Experience The ideal candidate will demonstrate: Extensive project management experience Budget management expertise Work planning experience Team leadership and coaching abilities Cross-cultural management skills Why This Opportunity Stands Out This position offers a unique opportunity to shape one of Africa s largest refugee and displaced youth inclusion programmes. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to: Influence policy and programming across multiple African countries. Contribute to improving opportunities for one million young people. Work with leading international development partners. Lead innovative approaches to refugee inclusion. Strengthen Refugee-Led Organizations across Africa. Drive systemic change in education and employment ecosystems. Additional Considerations Candidates should be aware that: The position may require periodic travel across Africa. Flexible working hours may occasionally be required. Criminal background verification may be conducted where appropriate. WUSC maintains a strict commitment to safeguarding, accountability, respect, and professional integrity. Application Information Applications are reviewed continuously on a rolling basis. Because the recruitment process may close once a suitable candidate has been identified, interested professionals are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. This is a rare opportunity for experienced bilingual development leaders to contribute to transformative change for refugee and displaced youth across Africa while working with one of the continent s most innovative and impactful international development initiatives. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post WUSC DREEM Deputy Project Director Job 2026: Lead a Pan-African Initiative Empowering One Million Refugee and Displaced Youth Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation (ReSET) Project 2026: Up to £20 Million Available for NGOs Supporting Refugees and Host Communities in Uganda
summary: Organizations working to improve the lives of refugees and host communities in Uganda now have an important opportunity to secure significant funding through the Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation (ReSET) Project. The British High Commission in Kampala, through the UK Foreign, Commonwealth Development Office (FCDO), has officially launched a call for proposals seeking experienced partners to help address humanitarian needs while building long-term resilience and self-reliance among refugee populations and host communities. With a total funding envelope of up to £20 million over a period of two and a half years, this initiative represents one of the most significant refugee-focused development funding opportunities currently available in Uganda. The programme is designed to support vulnerable refugee populations, strengthen host communities, and contribute to Uganda’s globally recognized refugee policy framework. Organizations interested in participating must submit their concept notes by 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM East Africa Time (EAT). About the Uganda ReSET Programme The Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation (ReSET) Programme is a bilateral partnership between the United Kingdom and the Government of Uganda. The initiative seeks to support Uganda’s transition from a predominantly humanitarian refugee response model toward a more sustainable approach centered on resilience, empowerment, and self-reliance. Uganda is internationally recognized for its progressive refugee policies, which provide refugees with rights and opportunities that are often unavailable in many other refugee-hosting countries. However, increasing refugee numbers and growing resource constraints have created significant challenges that require innovative and sustainable solutions. To address these challenges, the ReSET Programme focuses on helping refugees become more self-reliant while ensuring that urgent humanitarian needs continue to be met. The programme s business case was approved in March 2026, and activities funded through this call for proposals will run from August 2026 through March 2029. Funding Overview Key details of the funding opportunity include: Funding Organization: Foreign, Commonwealth Development Office (FCDO) through the British High Commission Kampala Programme Name: Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation (ReSET) Total Available Funding: Up to £20 million Implementation Period: August 2026 – March 2029 Duration: Approximately 2.5 years Geographic Focus: Uganda Eligible Applicants: NGOs registered under the Uganda NGO Bureau Deadline: 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM EAT Objectives of the ReSET Programme The ReSET Programme aims to create lasting positive change by supporting both immediate humanitarian assistance and long-term development outcomes. The initiative is built around two major pillars that organizations may choose to address individually or jointly. Pillar 1: Meeting Core Humanitarian Needs The first pillar focuses on ensuring that the most vulnerable refugees and affected host communities receive critical humanitarian support. This includes: Food Security Support Organizations may propose interventions that: Improve access to food for vulnerable populations Reduce food insecurity among refugee households Support nutrition-sensitive programming Address emergency food needs among newly arrived refugees Protection Services Projects may focus on: Child protection Gender-based violence prevention and response Legal protection services Community-based protection mechanisms Protection of vulnerable groups Multisectoral Humanitarian Assistance Applicants are encouraged to design integrated responses that combine multiple services to effectively address urgent humanitarian needs. Special attention should be given to: Newly arrived refugees Vulnerable women and girls Persons with disabilities Youth Host communities affected by humanitarian shocks Pillar 2: Building Self-Reliance and Resilience The second pillar focuses on helping refugees and host communities move beyond dependence on humanitarian assistance. The objective is to enable economically vulnerable households to build sustainable livelihoods and increase resilience against future shocks. Areas of intervention may include: Economic Empowerment Projects could support: Income generation Entrepreneurship Vocational training Financial inclusion Skills development Livelihood Strengthening Potential interventions include: Agricultural development Market access initiatives Value chain development Business incubation Employment creation Resilience Building Organizations may propose activities aimed at: Improving household resilience Strengthening community coping mechanisms Enhancing disaster preparedness Reducing vulnerability to economic and environmental shocks Cross-Cutting Themes and Priorities The British High Commission and FCDO emphasize that all projects should align with several important cross-cutting priorities. Gender Equality and Social Inclusion Applicants should demonstrate how their interventions will: Promote gender equality Empower women and girls Increase opportunities for marginalized groups Ensure inclusive participation Climate Resilience Projects should integrate climate-smart approaches and support communities in adapting to environmental challenges. Social Cohesion Successful proposals should strengthen positive relationships between refugees and host communities. Localization Organizations are encouraged to incorporate local leadership and community-driven solutions into project design and implementation. Alignment with Government Priorities Proposals should demonstrate alignment with: Government of Uganda policies National refugee frameworks International Financial Institution (IFI) priorities Existing development strategies Who Can Apply? The ReSET call for proposals is open to organizations that meet the following requirements: Eligible Organizations International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGOs) National Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) Refugee-Led Organizations (RLOs) Registration Requirement All applicants must possess valid registration under the Uganda NGO Bureau. Consortium Applications Encouraged The British High Commission welcomes applications from: Individual organizations Partnerships Consortia Consortium approaches may provide opportunities to combine expertise across multiple sectors and improve the overall impact of proposed interventions. How to Apply Organizations must submit an Expression of Interest through a concept note. Concept Note Requirements Applicants are required to submit: A concept note not exceeding seven pages A clear implementation strategy Expected outcomes and impacts Alignment with ReSET objectives Budget considerations Organizational capacity information Applicants May Propose Activities Under: Pillar 1 only – Core Humanitarian Needs Pillar 2 only – Self-Reliance and Resilience Both Pillar 1 and Pillar 2 Organizations should clearly indicate which pillar(s) their proposal addresses. Why This Funding Opportunity Matters Uganda hosts one of the largest refugee populations in Africa and continues to demonstrate leadership through its inclusive refugee policies. However, increasing humanitarian demands require innovative approaches that not only address immediate needs but also create sustainable pathways toward independence and resilience. Through the ReSET Programme, the UK Government aims to: Improve humanitarian outcomes Strengthen economic opportunities Enhance social cohesion Promote gender equality Build climate resilience Support long-term development solutions The programme represents a major investment in creating durable solutions for refugees and host communities while strengthening Uganda’s refugee response system. Key Benefits of the ReSET Funding Opportunity Organizations selected under this initiative may benefit from: Access to a funding pool worth up to £20 million Long-term implementation period of approximately 2.5 years Opportunity to influence refugee policy and development outcomes Support for both humanitarian and development programming Increased visibility through partnership with the UK Government Potential for large-scale impact among refugee and host populations Opportunities for collaboration through consortium approaches Important Deadline Organizations must submit their concept notes no later than: 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM East Africa Time (EAT) Late submissions may not be considered. Contact Information For questions and clarifications regarding the ReSET call for proposals, applicants may contact: bhckampala.submissions@fcdo.gov.uk Interested organizations are encouraged to begin preparing their concept notes immediately to ensure sufficient time for proposal development and submission before the deadline. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation (ReSET) Project 2026: Up to £20 Million Available for NGOs Supporting Refugees and Host Communities in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women – UN Women JPO Opportunity in Dakar, Senegal (2026 Intake)
summary: The Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women (EVAW) position under the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) programme with UN Women represents one of the most impactful early-career international development opportunities available in 2026. Based in Dakar, Senegal , this role is designed for young professionals who are committed to advancing gender equality, eliminating violence against women and girls, and contributing to global development efforts through the United Nations system. This opportunity is managed through the Nedworc Foundation , which coordinates Junior Professional Officer placements across various UN agencies. Applications for this prestigious role close on 28 June 2026 . Overview of the Opportunity The Programme Analyst position sits within UN Women’s Ending Violence Against Women (EVAW) Section , which focuses on designing and implementing programmes that address violence against women and girls across Africa. Violence against women and girls remains one of the most widespread human rights challenges globally, and this role contributes directly to regional and continental solutions. The selected candidate will work on: Programme planning and implementation Policy development and advocacy Multi-country and regional coordination Partnerships with governments, UN agencies, and civil society Monitoring and evaluation of gender-based violence programmes Support for large-scale global initiatives such as the Spotlight Initiative A major focus of the role is contributing to the Spotlight Initiative , a landmark partnership between the United Nations and the European Union aimed at eliminating all forms of violence against women and girls. Key Responsibilities The Programme Analyst will be expected to contribute to multiple areas of work within the EVAW Section: 1. Programme Development and Implementation Supporting the design of EVAW programmes across Africa Assisting in the implementation of regional and country-level initiatives Ensuring alignment with UN Women’s strategic objectives 2. Policy and Advocacy Work Supporting policy reforms related to gender equality Assisting governments in strengthening legal frameworks on gender-based violence Contributing to advocacy campaigns and awareness initiatives 3. Partnership Coordination Collaborating with UN agencies and development partners Engaging with civil society organizations and women’s rights groups Supporting multi-sectoral coordination efforts 4. Monitoring and Evaluation Collecting and analyzing programme data Assessing programme impact and effectiveness Contributing to reporting and documentation processes 5. Support to Global Initiatives Assisting in implementation of the Spotlight Initiative Supporting EVAW flagship programme development Contributing to multi-country programming strategies Development Focus Areas This role contributes to several interconnected development priorities across Africa: Ending Violence Against Women and Girls The primary focus is to prevent and respond to all forms of violence affecting women and girls, including: Domestic violence Sexual violence Harmful traditional practices Institutional and systemic gender inequality Reproductive and Maternal Health Collaboration The EVAW Section works with UN partners on broader health-related programming, including: Reproductive health access Maternal and neonatal health Child and adolescent health services Multi-Agency UN Collaboration The role also contributes to initiatives such as: Muskoka Initiative H6 partnership framework Cross-agency gender equality programming Why This Opportunity Matters The Junior Professional Officer programme is one of the most respected entry routes into the United Nations system. This role offers: Direct exposure to international development programming Hands-on experience with UN policy and field operations Opportunities to work with global experts and policymakers Experience in high-impact gender equality programming Strong career progression pathways in international organizations Application Deadline The closing date for applications is: 28 June 2026 Early application is strongly recommended due to high competition. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet strict eligibility criteria: Age Requirement Must be born after 1 January 1994 Must be 32 years or younger by 31 December 2026 Educational Requirements Applicants must hold either: Master’s Degree Gender Studies, Development Studies, Economics, Sociology, International Relations, or related fields Minimum 2 years relevant experience OR Bachelor’s Degree Minimum 4 years relevant professional experience Academic Background Requirement A significant portion of academic studies must have been completed in an eligible country listed by the programme Nationality Requirement Applicants must be nationals of eligible countries only Nationals of Senegal are not eligible due to the duty station being in Dakar Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for individuals who: Are passionate about gender equality and women’s rights Have experience in development, social policy, or human rights work Are interested in UN systems and international cooperation Have strong analytical and communication skills Are committed to addressing gender-based violence globally Eligible Applicants: Region and Background Geographic Eligibility Applications are open to candidates from: Eligible countries listed under the Nedworc Foundation JPO scheme Countries across Africa, Asia, Latin America, Eastern Europe, and other developing regions (excluding Senegal for this post) Professional Background Strong applicants typically come from: Gender Studies International Development Sociology Political Science Law or Human Rights Economics or Social Policy Application Learn more and apply here Category of Opportunity Job (Junior Professional Officer – UN Women Programme Analyst Position) Category of Qualified Candidates Early-career professionals (typically under 32 years old) Holders of Bachelor’s or Master’s degrees in social sciences or development-related fields Candidates from eligible countries listed under the UN JPO programme (excluding Senegal for this posting) Individuals with 2–4+ years of relevant professional experience depending on qualification level Professionals interested in gender equality, human rights, and international development careers For more information, visit the official website The post Programme Analyst, Ending Violence Against Women – UN Women JPO Opportunity in Dakar, Senegal (2026 Intake) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: How GiveWell Is Searching for the Best Ways to Help in 2026: A $500 Million Strategy to Save Lives Through Global Health, Nutrition, Water, Vaccination, and Poverty Reduction
summary: As global development challenges continue to affect millions of people worldwide, identifying the most cost-effective ways to save and improve lives remains one of the most important tasks in philanthropy. In 2026, GiveWell has unveiled an ambitious strategy aimed at directing at least $500 million toward the highest-impact opportunities in global health and development. The organization s research efforts are centered on two major goals: Expanding its research capacity to identify more highly effective programs capable of absorbing significant funding. Granting at least $500 million to interventions that can generate measurable improvements in health, survival, and well-being. To achieve these goals, GiveWell has doubled the size of its research team in recent years. The organization now employs approximately 60 researchers distributed across 11 specialized research units covering critical sectors such as malaria prevention, nutrition, vaccination, water quality, livelihoods, health systems strengthening, and emerging global health opportunities. This article explores GiveWell s comprehensive roadmap for 2026 and the strategies it plans to use to identify and fund life-saving interventions around the world. GiveWell s Research Expansion Strategy GiveWell s growing research infrastructure reflects its commitment to evidence-based philanthropy. Rather than focusing solely on proven interventions, the organization is increasingly investing in identifying new opportunities with the potential for large-scale impact. Its research structure now includes specialized teams dedicated to: Malaria Nutrition Vaccination Water Quality Livelihoods and Poverty Reduction New Global Health Areas Cross-Cutting Research Research Operations Commons (Research Review and Quality Assurance) Each team contributes to a broader mission of ensuring that donor funding reaches programs capable of generating the greatest possible benefit per dollar spent. Malaria: GiveWell s Largest Research Area Malaria remains one of the deadliest infectious diseases globally, causing approximately 600,000 deaths annually. Young children in Africa bear the greatest burden, accounting for roughly 70% of malaria-related deaths. Recognizing the scale of this challenge, GiveWell s malaria research division has become its largest research area, consisting of 15 specialists working across three major subteams. Antimalarial Medicines This team focuses on medicines used to prevent and treat malaria infections. Key Priorities for 2026 1. Optimizing Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) The team will continue supporting the highly successful SMC programs implemented by organizations such as Malaria Consortium while concentrating on a limited number of high-value research questions. 2. Expanding SMC Coverage Researchers will explore opportunities to increase malaria prevention coverage while evaluating alternative implementing partners. 3. Developing New Prevention Approaches Potential interventions include: Perennial malaria chemoprevention Post-malaria discharge prevention Preventive treatment for school-aged children Improved SMC delivery strategies 4. Improving Access to Treatment Following changes in global health funding, GiveWell has identified gaps in malaria treatment services and plans to continue funding interventions that improve access to diagnosis and treatment. Malaria Vector Control This team focuses on preventing malaria transmission by targeting mosquito populations. Major Research Priorities Expanding Insecticide-Treated Net Distribution Researchers are identifying regions with significant net shortages, particularly in high-burden countries where reductions in aid may have widened coverage gaps. Exploring New Mosquito-Control Technologies Potential future investments include: Routine mosquito net distribution systems Spatial repellents Insecticide-treated baby wraps Innovative vector-control technologies Strengthening Partner Capacity The team will assess and improve collaborations with key implementing organizations to maximize efficiency and impact. Malaria Cross-Cutting Research This group challenges existing assumptions and explores entirely new malaria solutions. Areas of Focus Funding innovative malaria pilot projects Supporting diagnostic market improvements Investigating malaria rebound effects Improving cost-effectiveness modeling Publishing reports that invite external expert review The goal is to continuously refine GiveWell s malaria funding strategy and uncover opportunities that traditional grantmaking channels may overlook. Nutrition: Fighting Hidden Hunger and Malnutrition Nutrition remains one of the most cost-effective areas for improving health outcomes in low- and middle-income countries. The Nutrition Team focuses on interventions targeting: Vitamin A deficiency Iron deficiency anemia Acute malnutrition Micronutrient deficiencies Vitamin A Supplementation Expansion GiveWell continues to support vitamin A supplementation programs where deficiency and child mortality rates remain high. In 2026, researchers will: Explore new geographic areas for expansion Conduct surveys and trials Improve estimates of intervention impact Addressing Anemia in Africa Anemia affects millions of children and women across Africa. The team is currently studying: Iron supplementation strategies Food fortification programs Multiple micronutrient supplementation The interaction between iron interventions and malaria infections Findings from this research may unlock major new funding opportunities across the continent. Reducing the Cost of Malnutrition Treatment Although acute malnutrition treatment can save lives, many programs currently fall below GiveWell s cost-effectiveness threshold. To address this challenge, GiveWell plans to: Consult implementers and donors Identify cost-saving innovations Fund pilot projects Test new treatment models Exploring Additional Health Opportunities Researchers are also investigating broader nutrition-related opportunities, including: Cardiovascular disease prevention Emerging public health interventions Future grantmaking pipelines Vaccination: Reaching the Last Mile Vaccines are among the most effective public health tools ever developed. Despite strong global funding, millions of children still miss essential vaccinations due to geographic and logistical barriers. Expanding Vaccination Outreach Programs GiveWell has already funded outreach initiatives in multiple countries. In 2026, the organization plans to: Launch additional vaccination programs Reach remote communities Conduct independent coverage surveys Evaluate program effectiveness Caregiver Incentive Programs Building on successful experiences with New Incentives, GiveWell is exploring programs that provide small financial incentives to encourage caregivers to vaccinate children. The organization aims to: Recruit new implementing partners Test innovative operational models Expand successful approaches Supporting Malaria Vaccine Rollout Researchers will evaluate opportunities to support: Malaria vaccine deployment Supply chain improvements Vaccine delivery innovations Research and development initiatives Water Quality: Tackling a Global Health Crisis More than one billion people still lack access to safe drinking water. Contaminated water contributes to hundreds of thousands of deaths annually through diseases such as: Diarrhea Cholera Dysentery Typhoid Exploring Alternative Water Treatment Technologies GiveWell is expanding beyond traditional chlorination programs. Technologies under consideration include: Ultraviolet purification systems Water filtration technologies Water kiosks Direct clean-water delivery systems Partnering with Development Banks A promising strategy involves integrating water treatment solutions into large-scale infrastructure projects financed by multilateral development institutions. If successful, this approach could unlock substantial future funding opportunities. Investing in Water Innovation Researchers are supporting: Improved contamination testing systems Low-cost chlorine sensors Market-shaping initiatives Innovation prizes These efforts aim to improve both effectiveness and scalability. Livelihoods: Reducing Extreme Poverty GiveWell has recently increased its focus on interventions that improve economic well-being. The organization is testing whether livelihoods programs deserve a larger share of future philanthropic funding. Cash Transfers Researchers are studying how cash transfers affect: Household consumption Housing quality Local economies Prices and wages The findings may help optimize future poverty-reduction programs. Ultra-Poor Graduation Programs These interventions combine: Productive assets Training Temporary financial support Savings mechanisms Evidence suggests they can create sustainable pathways out of extreme poverty. GiveWell is evaluating newer, potentially more cost-effective versions of these programs. Microfinance Innovation The team is exploring modern microfinance approaches that may produce stronger outcomes than earlier models. Research priorities include: Productive asset financing New targeting mechanisms Improved delivery models New Areas: Expanding Beyond Traditional Funding Priorities GiveWell is actively exploring health interventions that have received relatively little funding in the past. Potential focus areas include: Tuberculosis Medical oxygen Drug quality improvement Artificial intelligence for global health Hypertension HIV treatment Family planning Health systems strengthening The organization plans to increase grantmaking in these areas by approximately 20% compared with 2025. Leveraging AI and Advanced Research Methods One of GiveWell s most innovative priorities for 2026 is integrating artificial intelligence into its research workflow. Applications include: Literature reviews Evidence synthesis Research support Operational efficiency improvements The organization is also systematically evaluating AI performance to determine how future advances may improve philanthropic decision-making. Strengthening Research Quality and Operations As GiveWell scales its grantmaking, maintaining research quality remains essential. Supporting teams will focus on: Hiring and training researchers Evaluating past grants Monitoring and evaluation Knowledge management Process optimization Partner coordination These efforts are designed to ensure that larger funding volumes continue to produce meaningful impact. Conclusion GiveWell s 2026 strategy represents one of the most ambitious evidence-based philanthropy efforts currently underway. By combining rigorous research, innovative grantmaking approaches, emerging technologies, and a commitment to learning, the organization aims to direct at least $500 million toward interventions that can save lives and improve well-being on a massive scale. From malaria prevention and nutrition programs to safe drinking water, vaccination outreach, poverty reduction, and emerging health innovations, GiveWell s expanding research agenda demonstrates how data-driven philanthropy can identify opportunities capable of generating extraordinary social returns. As global funding uncertainties continue to challenge health and development programs worldwide, GiveWell s commitment to finding and supporting the most cost-effective interventions may play a critical role in improving millions of lives in 2026 and beyond. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post How GiveWell Is Searching for the Best Ways to Help in 2026: A $500 Million Strategy to Save Lives Through Global Health, Nutrition, Water, Vaccination, and Poverty Reduction appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program 2027 at Stanford University: Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship for Global Leaders Across All Disciplines
summary: Applications are now open for the Knight-Hennessy Scholars (KHS) Program 2027 at Stanford University in the United States. Recognized as one of the world’s most prestigious fully funded graduate scholarship and leadership development programmes, Knight-Hennessy Scholars supports outstanding students from around the world who are preparing to pursue graduate studies at Stanford University while developing as ethical, visionary, and globally minded leaders. The Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program is designed to build a multidisciplinary, multicultural community of future leaders committed to solving the world’s most complex challenges. Every year, up to 100 scholars are selected from across the globe to receive funding for graduate studies at Stanford University while participating in leadership development programmes, mentorship opportunities, experiential learning, and global networking activities. Unlike many traditional scholarships that focus primarily on academic performance, Knight-Hennessy Scholars evaluates applicants holistically. The programme seeks individuals who demonstrate independence of thought, purposeful leadership, and a civic mindset. The application process is therefore designed not only to assess academic excellence, but also to understand who applicants are, how they think, how they lead, and how they aspire to create positive impact in the world. The 2027 application cycle introduces detailed guidance for applicants regarding application preparation, leadership reflection, essays, recommendation letters, AI usage, interviews, and the invitation-only Finalist Experience. About the Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program Knight-Hennessy Scholars is Stanford University’s flagship graduate scholarship programme. The initiative brings together graduate students from all seven Stanford schools into a collaborative and interdisciplinary leadership community. The programme was founded to prepare a new generation of global leaders capable of addressing pressing challenges in: Public policy Science and technology Medicine and healthcare Business and entrepreneurship Education Law and governance Climate action Social innovation International development Artificial intelligence Sustainability Human rights Economic development Knight-Hennessy Scholars emphasizes leadership, service, innovation, ethical decision-making, and collaboration across cultures and disciplines. The programme provides: Full funding for graduate study Leadership training Professional development Global networking opportunities Community engagement Multidisciplinary collaboration Experiential learning Access to Stanford’s world-class ecosystem Why the Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program Stands Out The Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program is unique because it combines: Fully funded graduate education Leadership development Multidisciplinary learning Global community building Civic engagement Personal growth and reflection The programme recognizes that solving modern global problems requires leaders who can: Think critically Collaborate across disciplines Understand diverse perspectives Lead ethically Adapt to ambiguity Inspire positive change Every scholar joins a diverse cohort representing different: Countries Cultures Academic fields Professional backgrounds Lived experiences Leadership journeys The programme explicitly states that there is no “typical” Knight-Hennessy Scholar. Each cohort is intentionally diverse and multidisciplinary. Learn More Fully Funded Scholarship Benefits Knight-Hennessy Scholars receive extensive financial support to pursue graduate studies at Stanford University. The scholarship generally covers: Full tuition Living stipend Academic expenses Travel costs Leadership programme participation Professional development opportunities The programme also supports scholars through: Leadership training workshops Mentorship programmes Global networking Community-building events Cultural activities Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities In addition, finalists invited to the Finalist Experience receive covered travel expenses, including: Airfare Accommodation at Stanford Meals during the programme Local programme expenses Learn More Graduate Programmes Covered Knight-Hennessy Scholars supports students pursuing eligible full-time graduate degrees at Stanford University, including: MA MBA MS MFA MPP JD MD DMA PhD Other eligible graduate programmes Applicants must separately apply to both: Knight-Hennessy Scholars An eligible Stanford graduate degree programme The admissions processes are separate but concurrent. Knight-Hennessy Scholars Selection Criteria The programme evaluates applicants based on three core criteria. 1. Independence of Thought Knight-Hennessy Scholars seeks visionary thinkers who are: Curious Analytical Open-minded Reflective Creative Eager to learn from diverse perspectives Strong applicants demonstrate: Intellectual curiosity Clear thinking Problem-solving ability Comfort with ambiguity Innovative thinking Desire for continuous learning The programme values applicants who challenge assumptions and generate meaningful ideas to solve real-world problems. 2. Purposeful Leadership The programme seeks courageous leaders who: Inspire others Create meaningful impact Demonstrate resilience Lead ethically Make value-driven decisions Applicants should demonstrate: Initiative Persistence Leadership potential Positive influence Results-oriented action Commitment to meaningful change Leadership can be demonstrated in: Academic environments Community projects Workplaces Nonprofits Research initiatives Entrepreneurial ventures Personal experiences 3. Civic Mindset Knight-Hennessy Scholars values individuals who are: Collaborative Empathetic Humble Trustworthy Community-oriented Strong applicants often demonstrate: Service to others Commitment to social good Respect for diverse viewpoints Integrity Accountability Desire to improve society The programme strongly emphasizes community engagement and service-oriented leadership. Eligibility Requirements Knight-Hennessy Scholars has broad global eligibility and encourages applications from all nationalities. Requirement 1: Admission to Stanford Applicants must: Apply to and gain admission into a full-time Stanford graduate degree programme Enroll in the Stanford programme concurrently with Knight-Hennessy Scholars Eligible applicants include: New graduate applicants Applicants with deferred Stanford admission First-year Stanford PhD students Stanford students applying for a new eligible graduate degree Requirement 2: Undergraduate Degree Timeline Applicants must have earned their bachelor’s degree: In January 2020 or later for the 2027 cohort Military applicants receive an extended eligibility window to January 2018. Current undergraduate students are eligible if they will complete their first degree by September 2027. Learn More No Restrictions Based on Background The programme has: No age restrictions No nationality restrictions No quotas by country No quotas by discipline No quotas by academic field Applicants from all regions and academic disciplines are encouraged to apply. Application Components The Knight-Hennessy Scholars application is comprehensive and designed to understand applicants deeply. 1. Online Application Form Applicants must complete an online form that includes: Personal information Academic history Activities and achievements Employment background Awards and honors Leadership experiences The programme encourages applicants to provide detailed context rather than simplifying their experiences. 2. One-Page Resume Applicants must submit a one-page resume highlighting: Academic achievements Leadership experiences Professional activities Extracurricular involvement Key accomplishments The programme requests: Reverse chronological order No photographs No hyperlinks Legible formatting One US Letter-sized page only 3. Transcripts and Test Scores Applicants must upload: Undergraduate transcripts Graduate transcripts (if applicable) Degree conferral information If required by the Stanford graduate programme, applicants must also submit: GRE GMAT LSAT MCAT TOEFL IELTS PTE (MBA only) There is no minimum GPA or standardized test score requirement for Knight-Hennessy Scholars itself. 4. Recommendation Letters Applicants must submit two recommendation letters. Strong recommenders should: Know the applicant closely Provide specific examples Address the three selection criteria Demonstrate the applicant’s leadership and character The programme emphasizes that the content of the letter matters more than the recommender’s status or title. 5. Short Answers and Essay Applicants must complete several written responses. Short Answer Questions Applicants answer questions related to: Academic and professional goals Courage and resilience Working with diverse perspectives Moments of failure “Improbable facts” about themselves The programme explains that improbable facts should reveal surprising or unexpected aspects of the applicant’s identity rather than simply listing achievements. Main Essay Prompt Essay Topic: “Connect the dots. How have the influences in your life shaped you?” The programme encourages deep reflection and authenticity rather than simply describing accomplishments. Learn More Policy on AI Use in Applications Knight-Hennessy Scholars has established a detailed policy regarding AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Acceptable Uses Applicants may use AI for: Early brainstorming Organizing ideas Grammar checks Minor editing Unacceptable Uses Applicants may NOT use AI for: Drafting essays Generating substantive content Rewriting essays extensively Creating reflections or conclusions Replacing authentic personal voice The programme explicitly warns that overreliance on AI can weaken applications by obscuring the applicant’s authentic voice and independent thinking. Learn More Video Statement (Invitation Only) Approximately 500 applicants will receive invitations in January to submit a video statement. Video Prompt “Teach something to your cohort of fellow Knight-Hennessy Scholars.” Key guidelines include: Maximum length: 2 minutes Face must be visible No emphasis on production quality Topic can be academic or non-academic Authenticity matters most The programme encourages applicants to use the video as an opportunity to reveal personality, values, and communication style. Finalist Experience (Invitation Only) Around 180 finalists will participate in the Knight-Hennessy Finalist Experience. Components Include: Online individual interview Immersion Weekend at Stanford University Group activities Leadership exercises Networking events Group interviews Faculty presentations Immersion Weekend Dates March 5–6, 2027 Travel expenses are covered within programme guidelines. The Finalist Experience is designed to: Help applicants experience Stanford Assess interpersonal qualities Evaluate leadership and collaboration Facilitate meaningful interactions Important Application Dates Knight-Hennessy Scholars Application Opens June 1, 2026 Knight-Hennessy Scholars Deadline October 6, 2026 1:00 PM Pacific Time Video Invitations Rolling basis throughout January 2027 Finalists Announced January 27, 2027 Immersion Weekend March 5–6, 2027 Scholars Announced March 16, 2027 Reflection Questions Before Applying Knight-Hennessy Scholars encourages applicants to reflect deeply before applying.
